description: Assembled-junction forensics. Reads INFO/CONSENSUS + HOMLEN + HOMSEQ from DELLY/Manta PRECISE records that overlap the candidate's boundary zone. Distinct from mechanism.schema.json (which operates on reference-genome context via cheat29). When both blocks are present, this block is the preferred source of junction evidence — it describes the assembled rearrangement junction rather than the reference sequence at the estimated breakpoint.
question: Q4

q4b_asm_precise_record_available.description: Is there at least one PRECISE SV record (DELLY INV, DELLY BND pair, or Manta INV) spanning or near this candidate's boundary zones?

q4b_asm_source.description: Which caller/record class provided the evidence.

q4b_asm_homlen.description: INFO/HOMLEN from the chosen record — microhomology length at the ASSEMBLED junction, not at reference.

q4b_asm_consensus_available.description: Was INFO/CONSENSUS populated (assembled contig from split reads present)?

q4b_asm_junction_class.description: Junction classification from HOMLEN on the assembled sequence. blunt=0, MH_short=1-7, MH_long>=8, unresolved_no_precise_sv when no PRECISE record available.

q4b_asm_vs_ref_concordance.description: Does the assembled-junction class match cheat29's reference-based class? Discordance is meaningful evidence — it means cheat29 was reading the wrong position.

q4b_ref_junction_class.description: For convenience: cheat29's reference-based class when concordance was computed.
